rulings.Case Title: CFO RICK, INC VS. KRAEMER, NICK, AKA DOORMAN PROPERTY MANAGEMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Depositions Of Nick Kraemer And Barrett Raftery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CFO RICK, INC's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Nick Kraemer And Barrett Raftery. Pro Tem Judge Stephanie Krmpotic, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff/Cross-defendant CFO Rick, Inc.'s motion is granted subject to the following: CFO Rick, Inc. may take the depositions on or before May 24, 2018 conditioned upon payment of reasonable expenses and costs to each deponent for travel to a location as provided by CCP 2025.250(a). Alternatively, the parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding a date certain for the depositions to take place within the jurisdictions limits set for in CCP 2025.250(a) based on the dates range provided by counsel for Defendant/Cross-complainants; and shall meet and confer to continue the Defendant's/Cross-complainant's Motion for Summary Judgment to a date following completion of the depositions. Sanctions in the amount of $2,000 are awarded to moving party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jskrmpotic@lowball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS VS. BETTY YEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant California State Controller'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance Of Steven S. Rosenthal And/Or Motion For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 10, Defendant California State Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 10, Controller's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance Of Steven S. Rosenthal And/Or Motion For A Protective Order. Defendants' motion to quash the subpoena for the deposition of Steven Rosenthal is continued to July 9, 2018 to be heard concurrently with the parties' summary judgment/adjudication motions. If plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans believes that it needs the information it thinks it can obtain from the deposition of Mr. Rosenthal for its opposition to defendants' summary motion, Thrivent should make a CCP 437c(h) request that defendants' motion be denied or continued because the court has delayed hearing this motion. It is possible that the resolution of the summary motions will make it unnecessary for Mr. Rosenthal to be deposed and, even if not, the rulings on those motions will likely put in much greater focus than now whether the deposition should be allowed. Fidelity to the principles enunciated in Spectra-Physics and similar cases strongly suggests that the better course of action is to defer ruling on this motion until the summary motions have been decided. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA VS. SANTA BARBARA SAN LUIS OBISPO REGIONAL HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff Blue Shield Of Californias Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff California Physicians' Service's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued to June 13 2018. But for the Government Code 810.8 issue which the court is deferring to the June 13 hearing, Blue Shield has shown that it is entitled to judgment in its favor. 42 CFR 435.1015 creates a mandatory duty on CenCal not to make premium assistance payments where the cost of the premiums is substantially less expensive than the cost of providing direct coverage by CenCal. For purposes of Government Code 815.6, this mandatory duty is imposed by an enactment designed to protect private insurers such as Blue Shield from having an imbalanced risk pool with a disproportionately high number of severely ill insureds. The undisputed facts establish that CenCal violated this mandatory duty by paying premiums to Blue Shield for 40 of its Medi-Cal beneficiaries with end-stage renal failure who had not previously been insured by Blue Shield because the cost of providing direct coverage for those beneficiaries far exceeded the premiums. No later than May 25, 2018 defendant Santa Barbara San Luis Obispo Regional Health Authority may file a supplemental opposition memorandum limited to the issue of whether if the same premium assistance payments paid by CenCal were paid by a private health care organization those payments are actionable per the Unfair Competition Law, Business and Professions Code 17200, for purposes of Government Code 810.8. (See, e.g., Cel-Tech Communications, Inc. v. Los Angeles Cellular Telephone Co. (1999) 20 Cal. 4th 163). No later than June 5, 2018 Blue Shield may file a supplemental reply memorandum limited to the same issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Blue Shield is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA VS. SANTA BARBARA SAN LUIS OBISPO REGIONAL HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT SANTA BARBARA SAN LUIS OBISPO REGIONAL HEALTH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Santa Barbara San Luis Obispo Regional Health Authority's motion for summary judgment is continued to June 13 2018. But for the Government Code 810.8 issue which the court is deferring to the June 13 hearing, CenCal has not shown that it is entitled to judgment in its favor. 42 CFR 435.1015 creates a mandatory duty on CenCal not to make premium assistance payments where the cost of the premiums is substantially less expensive than the cost of providing direct coverage by CenCal. For purposes of Government Code 815.6, this mandatory duty is imposed by an enactment designed to protect private insurers such as Blue Shield from having an imbalanced risk pool with a disproportionately high number of severely ill insureds. The undisputed facts establish that CenCal violated this mandatory duty by paying premiums to Blue Shield for 40 of its Medi-Cal beneficiaries with end-stage renal failure who had not previously been insured by Blue Shield because the cost of providing direct coverage for those beneficiaries far exceeded the premiums. No later than May 25, 2018 plaintiff California Physicians' Service may file a supplemental opposition memorandum limited to the issue of whether if the same premium assistance payments paid by CenCal were paid by a private health care organization those payments are actionable per the Unfair Competition Law, Business and Professions Code 17200, for purposes of Government Code 810.8. (See, e.g., Cel-Tech Communications, Inc. v. Los Angeles Cellular Telephone Co. (1999) 20 Cal. 4th 163). No later than June 5, 2018 Blue Shield may file a supplemental reply memorandum limited to the same issue. No later than June 5, 2018 CenCal may file a supplemental reply memorandum limited to the same issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Blue Shield is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BERKLEY ASSURANCE COMPANY A CORPORATION VS. LAW OFFICES OF DAVID M. WISEBLOOD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joint Motion And Joint Motion To Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS MASHA LEVINSON, LEON TAYLOR'S, Joint Motion To Tax Costs. Defendants Masha Levinson and Leon Taylor's motion to tax costs is granted in its entirety. All costs claimed by plaintiff Berkley Assurance Company against Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor are taxed. Assuming without deciding that the motion was not timely filed per CRC 3.1700(b)(1), the court has authority per CRC 3.1700(b)(3) to extend the time for filing the motion by 30 days and does so here so that the motion is adjudicated on its merits. Per CCP 1032(a)(4), the court exercises its discretion to tax all claimed costs as to Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor for three reasons: 1) all or nearly all of those claimed costs would have been incurred as to other defendants whether or not Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor were named as defendants in this case; 2) this case was primarily against other defendants, not Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor; and 3) Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor appear to be the victims of other defendants' tortious behavior and, as a result of the court's rulings in this case, their losses will not be covered by the other defendants' insurance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor are required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KINSON WONG ET AL VS. BILL LEUNG, LEW, FONG, LEUNG, LEE & CHAN A et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT BILL LEUNG, LEW, FONG, LEUNG, LEE & CHAN MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Bill Leung, Lew Fong, Leung, Lee & Chan's motion to strike the entirety of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Kinson Wong is denied. While Mr. Wong should have filed his first amended complaint within the time frame set by the court, his failure to do so does not warrant the harsh consequence of precluding him from pursuing the claims alleged in the first amended complaint, especially since defendants have not identified any legal prejudice to them from the late filing. Denying the motion comports with California's strong policy in favor of deciding cases on their merits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Wong is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 5, Plaintiff Paige Barham'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, Privilege Log, Interrogatory Responses, Request For Admission Response, And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Stephanie Krmpotic, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel is granted as follow: Defendant is ordered to produce responsive documents, subject to further meet and confer regarding a protective order within 20-days. It is noted that certain requests may seek privileged/confidential information (RFPD 32, 36, 37, 40-42, 44, 49). Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Interrogatories is granted, except as to 4.1, 16.6, 16.7, 16.8, 20.3 and 20.4. Defendant is to provide responses based upon information currently in its possession within 20-days as of the date of the ruling. Sanctions in the amount of $3,000 are awarded to Plaintiff. rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC. A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's demurrer to the third, fourth, fifth and seventh causes of action assault, false imprisonment, intentional infliction of emotional distress and gender violence per Civil Code 52.4 in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jane Doe is sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for violation of section 52.4 and overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiff's allegations are sufficient to support a finding that Uber was an employer of Mr. Cortes and Mr. Cortes was acting in the course and scope of that employment. (Doe v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (ND CA 2016) 184 F. Supp. 3d 774; see also Dynamex Operations West, Inc. v. Superior Court (April 30, 2018) 2018 DAR 3856 (very recent California Supreme Court case suggesting that a company whose business is to drive vehicles such as Uber is the employer of its drivers, at least for some purposes)). Plaintiff has also alleged sufficient facts to support a claim for assault. Mr. Cortes' alleged conduct of driving the car to the side of the highway, his invitation to plaintiff to sit in the front seat, his pointing to his crotch while plaintiff was seated next to him, and his lewd comments supports an inference that the natural and probable consequence of his conduct would result in an offensive touching. Plaintiff has also alleged enough facts to support a claim for false imprisonment because a confinement against one's will can be effectuated by words, gestures or acts and plaintiff could reasonably perceive an implied threat of force in Mr. Cortes' conduct. Uber and Rasier are not liable for any alleged violation of section. 52.4 because there is no allegation that they personally committed the violence proscribed by that section, nor is it reasonably possible that plaintiff could make such an allegation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC. A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT GASPAR CORTES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Gaspar Cortes' demurrer to the second, third, fourth, fifth, and seventh causes of action for negligence per se, assault, false imprisonment, intentional infliction of emotional distress and gender violence per Civil Code 52.4 in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jane Doe is overruled in its entirety. The factual disputes raised by Mr. Cortes in his opposition are not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts for a claim of negligence per se and Penal Code 273 requires a general intent to commit the act of inflicting unjustified suffering on a child. Plaintiff has also alleged sufficient facts to support a claim for assault. Mr. Cortes' alleged conduct of driving the car to the side of the highway, his invitation to plaintiff to sit in the front seat, his pointing to his crotch while plaintiff was seated next to him and his lewd comments supports an inference that the natural and probable consequence of his conduct would result in an offensive touching. Plaintiff has also alleged enough facts to support a claim for false imprisonment because a confinement against one's will can be effectuated by words, gestures or acts and plaintiff could reasonably perceive an implied threat of force in Mr. Cortes' conduct. The alleged facts are also outrageous enough to support an IIED claim. Because assault is a crime that requires a threat of offensive touching, plaintiff has also alleged sufficient facts to support a claim for violation of section 52.4. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
